title: Zyak
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Zyak |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Well, Nomo, it's ''your'' shift. Sort this one out and you'll be a Captain by Monday. Fail, and you'll be working in the mines."
"Mines? What mines? Where will you go?"
"As far as I can. As far as I can.|Captain Zyak and Lieutenant Nomo as Fuel City's fuel cells are destroyed|''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''}} [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] '''Zyak''' was an officer of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] trained in air-traffic control. ==History== {{Quote|Look, flight control, I said I was sorry."
"Sorry doesn't count here in Fuel City, pilot.|[[Jan Ors]] and Captain Zyak|}} As of [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], he was stationed in [[Fuel City]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], where he considered he was wasting his life. While he was on duty, the [[HWK-290 light freighter/Legends|HWK-290 light freighter]] ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' came to the City and his scanners showed that it made an inexplicable stop at station six.[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] Zyak made a harsh remark to [[Jan Ors|the pilot]], ordering her to leave. The pilot claimed to have come off from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and was lost while looking for [[Barons Hed]]. In reality the pilot was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[agent/Legends|agent]] and she made the stop in order to let fellow agent [[Kyle Katarn]] to the station, and sneak into the ''[[Sulon Star]]''. Her voice was nice enough to mellow Zyak and advise her that [[Missile Battery|missile batteries]] would easily make a mistake after the [[Mission to Sulon|recent trouble]] in Barons Hed. Zyak said that he could have her permits revoked or ground her for violating [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] airspace; but offered to reduce her penalty after a "personal conference". The pilot showed to be in haste, and Zyak gave her thirty [[Standard second|seconds]] to disappear or his gunner would blow the ship up. Later that same day, he completed his shift and was replaced by [[Nomo]]; then he saw exchange of fire all around the station below his tower, and fragmentary comm traffic that came by talked about a [[Lightfight|firefight]] with an intruder. As Nomo was now responsible to handle the situation, he did nothing more than telling a stressful Nomo what to do, while watching the events with [[electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]]. He suggested telling the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|CO]] to be careful with their shots among the [[fuel/Legends|fuel]] tanks, and when he showed no compliance, he suggested to minimize circulation by closing the valves and bleeding the pumps. In all that chaos, a [[Unidentified air-traffic control technician|technician]] informed Zyak of an incoming ship; Zyak suggested to break off, but after a rude response, he scanned and saw again the ''Moldy Crow''. Just then he realized that the nice-voiced pilot was actually an agent who dropped the infiltrator off in the station, and as such was responsible for the battle that was being fought; Zyak ordered the ''Crow'' to be shot down. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Copy, one-niner-two. Just get that pile of junk off my screen. And be more careful in the future.|Captain Zyak to the ''Moldy Crow''|}} He had a thin mustache, and a sneer when on duty. He was disgusted with civilian [[pilot/Legends|pilots]] who often lost their ways or confused orders. However whenever he heard a nice female voice he would try to be a bit more polite. He lamented that because of his post, which comprised mostly of voice communication, he often would never got to see whom those nice voices belonged to. Even thought he thought Nomo was less than capable, he thought he was not obliged to show him some things after the end of his own duty; although later he decided to stay when the trouble began in Fuel City. Following the near-destruction of Fuel City, Zyak decided to go AWOL rather than face his superiors' wrath, even though [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Nomo]] had been on duty. ==Behind the scenes== In the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'']], he was voiced by [[Tim Russell]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' {{1st}} *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Imperial Navy captains of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
